requirements_conformance.evidence: CommitVersion calls WriteVersion (which fsync's tmp file then renames before returning) synchronously BEFORE any CAS attempt, so durability is literally complete prior to the CAS attempt (not merely prior to CAS success), satisfying the acceptance criteria's exact wording. CompareAndSwapCurrentVersion returns false+unchanged on expected mismatch, true+updated record on match.

architecture_conformance.evidence: engine/catalog/catalog.go's CompareAndSwapCurrentVersion holds the per-fileID stripe lock (c.stripes[stripeFor(fileID)]) across the ENTIRE read (readSlot/Decode) -> check (CurrentVersion==expected) -> write (tombstone+insert+index update) sequence via a single lock/defer-unlock, matching docs/LLD/mvcc.md's Write-path CAS description and reusing the same lock Get/Put already serialize on for that fileID -- no release-then-reacquire gap. However docs/LLD/mvcc.md states 'every version-pointer CAS ... goes through the WAL first', and this subtask's CAS/CommitVersion do NOT touch WAL at all. This is an intentional, previously-documented deferral (architecture-discovery.md notes it explicitly, consistent with 2a.1.1's own deferral of the same wiring, and issue #6's subtask breakdown assigns WAL wiring to 2a.1.4), so it is not a defect for 2a.1.2's stated scope, but it is a live architecture gap: CommitVersion is a real, callable production entry point today with no WAL durability for the CAS, so it must not be wired into any caller path before 2a.1.4 lands.

regression_risk.evidence: CompareAndSwapCurrentVersion's tombstone+insert sequence mirrors the pre-existing, already-verified Put implementation exactly (same stripe-lock/indexMu pattern), introducing no new locking model. Full suite go test ./... -race -count=1 passes (engine, engine/btree, engine/catalog, engine/mvcc, engine/wal all green); engine/catalog -race -v -count=1 passes with no new failures.

edge_cases.evidence: Correctness argument for 'final CurrentVersion == highest version file number on disk' is sound: WriteVersion's per-fileID monotonic, never-reused numbering means the goroutine that wrote the globally-last version file cannot lose its CAS (a loss would force it to write a strictly higher-numbered file, contradicting it being last), so it must be the eventual winner. This is a genuine proof, not just an assumption, GIVEN 2a.1.1's monotonicity guarantee holds (previously verified). No formal starvation-freedom/termination bound is given for the retry loop under adversarial scheduling (a purely theoretical, not practical, gap common to CAS-retry designs); worth a one-line doc note but not a blocker. Orphaned (losing-attempt) version files are never re-pointed-to since CurrentVersion only advances via exact-match CAS and version numbers never repeat/decrease; read-path exposure is a non-issue since no read path exists yet (2a.1.3).

security.evidence: No new external inputs, auth, or trust boundaries introduced. InvalidFileID is rejected up front.

performance.evidence: Lock scope is the same narrow per-fileID stripe already used by Put/Get; no new global lock. CAS retry writes one extra version file per lost race, which is inherent to the stated design (accepted GC-later cost per docs/LLD/mvcc.md's Garbage collection section).

maintainability.evidence: Extensive, accurate doc comments on both CompareAndSwapCurrentVersion and CommitVersion correctly explain the concurrency contract and cross-reference the LLD.

test_coverage.evidence: TestCurrentVersionCAS in engine/mvcc/write_test.go uses 30 real goroutines racing CommitVersion on one fileID, run under -race, and verifies: (1) every goroutine's CommitVersion call returns nil error and a distinct, non-zero version (no silent drops/collisions) -- len(seen)==30 explicitly checked, directly proving ALL N writers' data survived, not just the winner's; (2) at least 30 version files exist on disk (countVersionFiles scans the real content dir, counting orphaned files too); (3) final CurrentVersion equals the max version number on disk; (4) the file CurrentVersion points at contains exactly the specific winning writer's payload (no torn/corrupted content). Re-ran TestCurrentVersionCAS with -race -count=10: zero flakes across 10 runs (~27s total). go build ./... and go vet ./... clean (run from engine/ module directory, since repo uses go.work with api/ and engine/ as separate modules -- go build ./... from repo root fails with a go.work module-prefix error; this is a build invocation nuance, not a code defect).

confidence.evidence: Design, code, and test all agree; correctness argument for the CAS-retry invariant is checked and holds; full regression suite green; WAL gap is pre-acknowledged and correctly out of scope.

possible_improvements: Add a one-line comment in CommitVersion's doc acknowledging the retry loop has no formal per-goroutine termination bound under adversarial scheduling (progress is guaranteed system-wide via the monotonic-version argument, but not per-caller fairness) -- purely a documentation nit.

Consider asserting in a follow-on subtask/handoff note that CommitVersion must not be wired into any caller until 2a.1.4's WAL-before-CAS wiring lands, to prevent an accidental production integration gap between 2a.1.2 and 2a.1.4.

test_results.full_suite_race_count1: pass (engine, engine/btree, engine/catalog, engine/mvcc, engine/wal all ok)
